findings.title: Input acknowledgements are not correlated to the in-flight submission
findings.detail: In DispatchBriefing, any EvInputAck that is not the last already-acked id advances the session to Working and cancels the input timer. The code never checks ev.InputID against s.BriefInput, so a stale ack, an ack from a prior retry, or a misrouted ack from another session can falsely confirm the current brief/resume input. This violates RSM-027's correlation requirement and can let a resumed run proceed past an unconfirmed prompt.
findings.suggested_fix: Require a non-empty ev.InputID equal to the currently expected s.BriefInput before transitioning to Working. Treat mismatched acks as no-ops or rejection/stale inputs according to the agent-input contract, and add tests for stale, wrong-session, and wrong-input ack events.

findings.title: Runexec reimplements the agent-input ack timeout
findings.detail: The Dispatch reactor arms its own TimerInputAck after delivering input and later converts that timer into input_undeliverable. RSM-027 says the bounded output-or-stale window belongs to the agent-input seam and the reactor must not re-implement it; the shell should convert SubmitInput's ack plus durable agent_input_acked/agent_input_stale events into reactor events. Keeping a separate runexec timer can race or disagree with the input substrate's own ack/stale terminal and produce false fail-closed runs.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the runexec-owned input ack deadline from the reactor vocabulary, consume explicit agent-input ack/stale/rejected events from the shell, and update tests to prove runexec does not impose an independent input timeout.

findings.title: Rejected outcome emissions discard the classified failure reason
findings.detail: For gate failures, the rejected outcome prefix is emitted with Detail set only to "rejected"; merge/code-sync failures use the same pattern later in mergeExhaustedOrFatal. RSM-034 requires the rejected outcome emission to carry the classified reason before the reopen. The current Action has no field preserving ev.Reason or the composed merge failure reason for the effector, so downstream durable outcome_emitted records cannot include the failure classification the spec requires.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the emit action payload to distinguish outcome status from failure reason, populate it from ev.Reason for gate failures and from mergeFailureStrings for merge/code-sync failures, and update the A1 tests that currently assert only Detail == "rejected".
